topic: Aspects of Political Life in the French-speaking World
subject: french

items.q: How do you say 'Currently, I am using social media' in French?
items.why: Students avoid actuellement thinking it means 'actually'. It really means CURRENTLY. Useful for tech/social media topics. To say 'actually' (in reality), use 'en fait' or 'en réalité'.
Students assume librairie = library. It actually means BOOKSHOP. The French word for library is bibliothèque. Both relate to books but serve different purposes.

items.correct: As a nasal vowel - the n is not fully pronounced.
items.why: Students pronounce nasal vowels like English consonants. French 'on', 'an', 'in', 'un' are nasalised vowels - air resonates through the nose but the n consonant itself isn't articulated.

items.q: In which year did Algeria gain independence from France?
items.why: It's 1962. “1954” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which is NOT part of the Maghreb?
items.why: Correct answer: Sénégal. “Algérie” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year was Quebec's Law 101 (Charter of the French Language) passed?
items.why: Exact answer: 1977. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
